tips: Use f-strings for readable string interpolation: f'Hello {name}'
Use type hints to make code self-documenting and IDE-friendly
List comprehensions are faster and more readable than map/filter
Use pathlib.Path instead of os.path for cross-platform path handling
Context managers (with statement) ensure proper resource cleanup
Use dataclasses to reduce boilerplate in data-holding classes
Prefer tuple unpacking over indexed access for paired data
Use enumerate() instead of range(len()) when you need both index and value
Virtual environments isolate dependencies per project
Use zip() to iterate over multiple sequences in parallel
Use generators (yield) for memory-efficient iteration over large datasets
The walrus operator (:=) assigns and evaluates in a single expression
